blockingIssues.description: Consuming `self` receivers are still not enforced for trait-bound generic dispatch, so non-`Copy` bindings can still be reused after by-value trait-method calls.
Enum-payload partial-move rejection is still incomplete because built-in `Option`/`Result` payload bindings and borrowed-`self` match scrutinees bypass the new ownership guard.
Specialized `Ok(...)`, `Err(...)`, `Some(...)`, and `None` return fast paths still bypass the new nested cleanup and guard-suppression logic, leaving value-producing return cleanup incomplete.
Loop `break`/`continue` cleanup depth is still off, so `Drop` bindings declared in loop bodies can skip cleanup on those early exits.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify and enforce `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` reporting when a handoff explicitly records a tests-first or other documented skill deviation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `ownership-cleanup-lexical-scope-and-guard-suppression-completeness` again found a handoff that set `followedProcedure: true` while also recording a `Test-first sequencing` deviation, repeating the same reporting ambiguity already seen in round 1.
